threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think wearing trainers to a pub is fine—until you see everyone else in proper shoes sipping their gin like it's an art form.
Caught a couple arguing about their cat's name on the 36. Turns out it was a different cat than the one they broke up over last week.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a whole family of ducks waddling confidently through a corner café, pecking at crumbs while everyone just smiled. Classic Neukölln!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, I swear half the conversations are about the next BVG strike. It’s like a local sport—predicting when and where the chaos will hit next.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local if they can pinpoint the exact time of day to avoid the 24th Street BART station chaos—it's a rush hour symphony of street vendors and techies.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 while a guy convinced the driver to let him off mid-route. Only in San Francisco do Muni stops become suggestions.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into a plaça and find a table immediately. Meanwhile, we know it's a game of patience—locals snag spots way ahead!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're recent by the way they still order ' tapas' with a soft 't' instead of the strong 't' locals use. It’s a dead giveaway.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the bus stop instead of just looking for the nearest corner coffee shop with Wi-Fi.
Heard a bloke on the 35 brag about how he’s convinced the driver only takes the long route to avoid traffic, not the official one.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a whole family of raccoons digging through the bins at Hermannplatz while an old man serenaded them with an accordion.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings with no sign or schedule—just music pouring into the night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they navigate the BART delays without missing a beat, while newcomers just stare blankly at their apps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I saw a guy serenading a group on the 47 bus, turning it into a mini concert. Only in the Tenderloin do we turn delays into good vibes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think all of Gràcia is trendy bars and markets, but the best tapas spots are tucked away in tiny streets where locals fuss over recipes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they take selfies in front of every single Gaudí building, even the tiniest ones that locals just walk past.
